# UC-21.6.14 · Geo-User Session Tracking and Regional Conversion Funnels

## Description

Summarizes, for each geographic region, how many distinct browsing sessions touch cart, checkout, and confirmation URL patterns so you can see where multi-step shopping flows thin out relative to overall traffic landing on the storefront.

## Value

Revenue-focused teams localize fixes—payments, duties messaging, sizing tables, warehouse cutoffs—toward regions that pay for traffic but stall before purchase, tightening return on advertising spend faster than aggregated global dashboards allow.

## Implementation

Guarantee a stable session cookie field (`JSESSIONID` illustrated—swap for your storefront key). Correlate optionally with `checkout:event` rows keyed on the same session for higher-fidelity confirmations. Exclude staff and partner subnets. Document URL patterns per platform (Salesforce Commerce Cloud, Shopify, custom) inside a macro so merchandising changes do not silently break funnel stages.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Cookie names and URL taxonomy documented with growth and merchandising teams; macros for path regex stored in the search app.
• HEC token and index permissions for optional `checkout:event` correlation if you extend beyond access logs alone.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Front load balancers must pass the real client IP. Hash or drop personally identifying query strings at ingest. If multiple brands share an index, add `brand` or `host` as an additional split-by dimension in the search.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL (replace `JSESSIONID` with your session field):

```spl
index=web sourcetype=access_combined
| eval region=coalesce(clientip_country, geoip_country, "unknown")
| eval hit_cart=if(match(uri_path, "(?i)/cart") OR match(uri_path, "(?i)cart\\."), 1, 0)
| eval hit_checkout=if(match(uri_path, "(?i)/checkout"), 1, 0)
| eval hit_confirm=if(match(uri_path, "(?i)/confirm") OR match(uri_path, "(?i)/thank-you"), 1, 0)
| stats
    dc(eval(if(hit_cart==1,JSESSIONID,null()))) as sessions_cart
    dc(eval(if(hit_checkout==1,JSESSIONID,null()))) as sessions_checkout
    dc(eval(if(hit_confirm==1,JSESSIONID,null()))) as sessions_confirm
    dc(JSESSIONID) as sessions_any
  by region
| where sessions_any > 50
| eval cart_rate=round((sessions_cart/sessions_any)*100,2)
| eval checkout_rate=round((sessions_checkout/sessions_any)*100,2)
| eval confirm_rate=round((sessions_confirm/sessions_any)*100,2)
| sort - sessions_any
```

Understanding this SPL

**Geo-User Session Tracking and Regional Conversion Funnels** — counts unique sessions that ever touch each funnel stage, per region, using path-based intent labels.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• `eval hit_*` flags visits that satisfy regex URL intent.
• `dc(eval(if(...)))` counts distinct sessions per stage without double-counting multi-hit noise.
• Ratio fields convert raw counts into comparable rates for leadership slides.

Step 3 — Validate
Reconcile counts with your commerce analytics export for a single day and region. If discrepancies exceed a few percent, revisit bot filtering and session timeout definitions (max inactivity) so behavior matches the vendor tool you trust.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Publish weekly reviews with merchandising; pair drops with known campaigns. When a region regresses, open child investigations for payment errors, shipping messaging, or checkout translation bugs. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar funnel, Table with week-over-week sparklines, Optional join to order revenue per region for revenue overlay.

## SPL

```spl
index=web sourcetype=access_combined
| eval region=coalesce(clientip_country, geoip_country, "unknown")
| eval hit_cart=if(match(uri_path, "(?i)/cart") OR match(uri_path, "(?i)cart\\."), 1, 0)
| eval hit_checkout=if(match(uri_path, "(?i)/checkout"), 1, 0)
| eval hit_confirm=if(match(uri_path, "(?i)/confirm") OR match(uri_path, "(?i)/thank-you"), 1, 0)
| stats
    dc(eval(if(hit_cart==1,JSESSIONID,null()))) as sessions_cart
    dc(eval(if(hit_checkout==1,JSESSIONID,null()))) as sessions_checkout
    dc(eval(if(hit_confirm==1,JSESSIONID,null()))) as sessions_confirm
    dc(JSESSIONID) as sessions_any
  by region
| where sessions_any > 50
| eval cart_rate=round((sessions_cart/sessions_any)*100,2)
| eval checkout_rate=round((sessions_checkout/sessions_any)*100,2)
| eval confirm_rate=round((sessions_confirm/sessions_any)*100,2)
| sort - sessions_any
```

## Visualization

Stacked bar chart (funnel stages by region), Parallel coordinates plot (sessions_any through confirmation), Table (rates and deltas versus global baseline).

## Known False Positives

Regions where shoppers prefer mobile wallet deep links may skip labeled checkout paths yet still convert. Omnichannel buys that finish in-store under-represent confirmations in web logs alone. Affiliate tracking parameters lengthen URLs and can confuse naïve substring matches.
